description.es:
El [atributo global](/es/html/Global-Attributes/) `[accesskey](/es/html/Global-Attributes/accesskey/)` en [html](/es/html/) permite a los desarrolladores web especificar un atajo de teclado para activar o enfocar un elemento. Como [atributo global](/es/html/Global-Attributes/), puede aplicarse a cualquier elemento [html](/es/html/), proporcionando un mecanismo de navegación rápido para los usuarios que prefieren la interacción por teclado al ratón. No tiene un elemento padre específico, ya que su naturaleza global significa que se aplica a toda la estructura del documento.

El propósito principal de `[accesskey](/es/html/Global-Attributes/accesskey/)` es mejorar la accesibilidad y la usabilidad, permitiendo a los usuarios activar acciones o navegar a elementos utilizando una combinación de teclas (por ejemplo, `Alt` + tecla en Windows, `Ctrl` + `Alt` + tecla en Firefox, `Ctrl` + `Option` + tecla en Mac). Los casos de uso prácticos incluyen la asignación de atajos a acciones comunes como botones de \
description.en:
The `[accesskey](html/Global-Attributes/accesskey/)` [Global-Attribute](html/Global-Attributes/) in [html](/html/) allows web developers to specify a keyboard shortcut to activate or focus an element. As a [Global-Attribute](html/Global-Attributes/), it can be applied to any [html](/html/) element, providing a quick navigation mechanism for users who prefer keyboard interaction over a mouse. It does not have a specific parent element, as its global nature means it applies across the entire document structure.

The primary purpose of `[accesskey](html/Global-Attributes/accesskey/)` is to enhance accessibility and usability by enabling users to trigger actions or navigate to elements using a single key combination (e.g., `Alt` + key on Windows, `Ctrl` + `Alt` + key on Firefox, `Ctrl` + `Option` + key on Mac). Practical use cases include assigning shortcuts to common actions like "Submit" buttons, navigation links, or form fields.

While `[accesskey](html/Global-Attributes/accesskey/)` can improve efficiency for some users, its implementation requires careful consideration. Potential implications include conflicts with existing browser shortcuts or assistive technology keybindings, which can lead to a frustrating user experience. Best practices suggest avoiding common keys (like `s` for save, `p` for print) and instead using less common or context-specific keys. It's also crucial to inform users about available shortcuts, perhaps through tooltips or a dedicated help section. Browser support is generally good, but the specific modifier keys required vary, making consistent cross-browser behavior a challenge. Therefore, many developers prefer to implement custom keyboard shortcuts using JavaScript for greater control and predictability.

info.excerpt: This feature is well established and works across many devices and browser versions. It’s been available across browsers since July 2015.

description.en: Non-standard:This feature is not standardized. We do not recommend using non-standard features in production, as they have limited browser support, and may change or be removed. However, they can be a suitable alternative in specific cases where no standard option exists.
